Bash("/c/Users/Maximilian Sperlich/miniconda3/Scripts/conda.exe" run -n the-grove python -c "
from PIL import Image
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np

source_dir = Path(''src/the_grove_22/twigs/EuropeanOakTwig/textures'')
for tex_file in sorted(source_dir.glob(''OakEuropean*.png'')):
    img = Image.open(tex_file)
    print(f''{tex_file.name}: mode={img.mode}, has_alpha={""A"" in img.getbands()}'')
    if ''A'' in img.getbands():
        alpha_band = img.split()[-1]
        alpha_array = np.array(alpha_band)
        print(f''  Alpha range: {alpha_array.min()}-{alpha_array.max()}, mean: {alpha_array.mean():.1f}'')
")

Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Add bark textures and seasonal preference to twig export

Two enhancements to twig texture handling:

1. BARK TEXTURE SUPPORT: Paper birch and other twigs with separate bark
   textures now export correctly. Bark detected via filename and exported
   as separate material in USD files.
   - Updated classify_texture_from_name() to identify bark/bark_top/bark_bottom
   - Updated OPAQUE_TEXTURE_TYPES in both skeletal and static exports
   - Added bark texture connection in shader node creation

2. SEASONAL PREFERENCE: When multiple seasonal variants exist (summer/fall/
   winter/spring), summer is now always preferred, consistent with top/bottom
   texture preference pattern.
   - Improved material grouping to analyze seasonal keywords
   - Consolidate variants: if summer exists, remove fall/winter/spring
   - Added clear season prioritization logic

These changes ensure paper birch bark is included in exports and all
seasonal textures use consistent preferences across all species.

Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Improve alpha texture detection using corner-based analysis

Replace histogram-based alpha inversion detection with corner-based detection.
This is much more reliable because:
1. Actual geometry doesn''t extend to texture edges (corners are always background)
2. Corners reliably represent the "transparent" value, not entire image distribution
3. Fixes issues like silver fir where alpha texture has specific edge patterns

Corner detection analyzes 10x10 patches at each corner and checks if mean value
is bright (>155/255) or dark (<100/255). This determines which value represents
the transparent background, and inverts if corners are bright.

Applied to both texture_utils normalize_alpha_texture() and twig_export 
_detect_alpha_inversion(), ensuring consistent detection throughout pipeline.

Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")
